reasoning: The Indeed US Job Postings Index has exhibited a consistent downward trend over the past year (dropping from ~119 in April 2024 to ~107 in April 2025), reflecting a cooling labor market. The very recent 30-day linear trend is also slightly downward (-0.024 per day). Because of this persistent macro trend, the probability of the index increasing relative to the April 13th baseline is below 0.5 for all horizons. However, over longer horizons (3 to 6 months), epistemic uncertainty dominates: the labor market cooling may bottom out and reverse as conditions (e.g., interest rates or economic growth) adapt. Following the constraint that uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, the probabilities progressively approach 0.5 for the more distant dates, starting from 0.40 for 1 week out and rising to 0.49 for 6 months out.
